name: worlds-import
description: How to import a downloaded world/map zip into an instance and diagnose a "missing" world. Use when the user has a save zip to add or thinks a world disappeared ("导入地图","导入存档","存档丢了","地图没了","import world").
body:
# Worlds import

## Call
- `instance(action=worlds_import, instance, zip)` — imports a downloaded world/map zip into that
  instance's saves.

## Gotchas
- Safety net (automatic, no action needed): an imported world is marked for a one-time safety
  backup that `launch_instance` takes automatically right before that world's FIRST launch after
  import — protects an old/incompatible save from being silently truncated the first time
  Minecraft opens it. This is a code-level guarantee (see `WorldBackupManager`), not something you
  need to call yourself — don't run `worlds_backup_create` "just in case" right after an import
  unless the user specifically asks for an extra manual snapshot.
- Getting the zip onto disk isn't this tool's job — ask the user for the path (or the download
  URL) first.
- If it refuses, a zip-slip guard tripped: layout is wrong. It must contain a world folder with
  `level.dat` at the zip's top level or one directory down. Re-zip and retry.
- "存档不见了/坏了" is usually the wrong-instance illusion, not real loss: each instance can have
  its OWN saves dir (version isolation). Run `instance(action=worlds_list, instance)` on the
  instance the user expects — don't guess paths. Then check
  `instance(action=worlds_backup_list, instance, world)` and the OS recycle bin.
- Moving a world to another PC = copy the world folder (or a backup snapshot);
  `instance(action=open_folder)` helps the user find it.
